title: libertia peregrinans
intro: Libertia peregrinans, also known as the New Zealand iris or coastal lilyturf, is a flowering plant native to the coastal regions of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Libertia peregrinans has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Libertia peregrinans is often used as a tincture, tea, or dried root to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
